<file name="f:\AUDIRA-BUG-HUTTER\services\api\src\ai\ai.controller.ts" language="typescript" >
<content>
import { Controller, Post, Body, UseGuards, Request } from '@nestjs/common';
import { AIService } from './ai.service';
import { AnalyzePayloadDto } from './dto/ai.dto';
import { AuthGuard } from '../auth/guards/auth.guard';

@UseGuards(AuthGuard)
@Controller('ai')
export class AIController {
  constructor(private readonly aiService: AIService) {}

  @Post('analyze')
  async analyzePayload(@Body() dto: AnalyzePayloadDto, @Request() req: any) {
    return this.aiService.analyzeVulnerability(dto.vulnerabilityId, req.user.sub);
  }
}

</content>
</file>
<file name="f:\AUDIRA-BUG-HUTTER\services\api\src\ai\ai.service.ts" language="typescript" >
<content>
import { Injectable, NotFoundException } from '@nestjs/common';
import { PrismaService } from '../database/prisma.service';
import OpenAI from 'openai';

@Injectable()
export class AIService {
  private openai!: OpenAI;
  private isMockEnabled = true;

  constructor(private readonly prisma: PrismaService) {
    const apiKey = process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY;
    if (apiKey && !apiKey.startsWith('sk-proj-xxx')) {
      this.openai = new OpenAI({ apiKey });
      this.isMockEnabled = false;
    }
  }

  async analyzeVulnerability(vulnerabilityId: string, userId: string) {
    const vul = await this.prisma.vulnerability.findUnique({
      where: { id: vulnerabilityId },
      include: { target: true },
    });

    if (!vul) {
      throw new NotFoundException('Target vulnerability record not found');
    }

    const systemPrompt = `You are VulnX AI, a premier elite cybersecurity assistant. Analyze the following target vulnerability and supply a structured response in beautiful markdown containing details:
    1. EXPLOIT VECTOR ANALYSIS: Deconstruct how this exploit works mathematically or programmatically.
    2. ATTACK PAYLOAD EXAMPLES: Provide functional, educational proof-of-concept payload codes.
    3. PENETRATION METHODOLOGY: Detail standard red-team methods to confirm this finding.
    4. ENTERPRISE REMEDIATION: Step-by-step instructions to patch the vulnerability.`;

    const userPrompt = `Vulnerability Details:
    - Title: ${vul.title}
    - Type: ${vul.type}
    - Severity: ${vul.severity}
    - CVSS: ${vul.cvssScore}
    - Asset Target Scope: ${vul.target.scope}
    - Short Description: ${vul.description}`;

    if (this.isMockEnabled) {
      return {
        explanation: this.getMockResponse(vul),
        provider: 'vulnx-local-mock-model',
      };
    }

    try {
      const completion = await this.openai.chat.completions.create({
        model: 'gpt-4-turbo-preview',
        messages: [
          { role: 'system', content: systemPrompt },
          { role: 'user', content: userPrompt },
        ],
      });

      return {
        explanation: completion.choices[0].message.content,
        provider: 'openai-gpt-4',
      };
    } catch (e: any) {
      return {
        explanation: `### AI Service Connection Interrupted\nFailed to establish AI stream link.\n\n**Fallback Mock Diagnostics:**\n${this.getMockResponse(vul)}`,
        provider: 'local-fallback',
        error: e.message,
      };
    }
  }

  private getMockResponse(vul: any): string {
    return `## VulnX AI Security Synthesis — Target Ref: ${vul.id}

### 1. EXPLOIT VECTOR ANALYSIS
The vulnerability **${vul.title}** presents a critical risk factor. Exploiting **${vul.type}** on scope target \`${vul.target.scope}\` permits arbitrary logic processing bypass because of unvalidated context filtering inside input buffers.

### 2. ATTACK PAYLOAD EXAMPLES
For educational analysis, verification of ${vul.type} can be staged using standard probe payloads:
\`\`\`bash
# Diagnostic validation query
curl -X GET "${vul.target.scope}/vulnerable-endpoint?param=1' OR '1'='1" -H "User-Agent: VulnX-Sec-Scanner"
\`\`\`

### 3. REMEDIATION STRATEGY
* **Statically Bound Queries**: Implement fully prepared statement architectures inside all database integration packages.
* **Aggressive Input Encoding**: Sanitize incoming parameters utilizing safe validation modules prior to context loading.
* **Strict Network Filtering**: Establish proper Web Application Firewall (WAF) rule sets to filter malicious characters like \`'\`, \`"\`, \`<\`, \`>\`.
`;
  }
}

</content>
</file>
<file name="f:\AUDIRA-BUG-HUTTER\services\api\src\ai\dto\ai.dto.ts" language="typescript" >
<content>
import { IsNotEmpty, IsUUID } from 'class-validator';

export class AnalyzePayloadDto {
  @IsNotEmpty()
  @IsUUID(4, { message: 'Vulnerability reference must be a valid UUID v4' })
  vulnerabilityId!: string;
}

</content>
</file>

<file name="f:\AUDIRA-BUG-HUTTER\apps\web\app\layout.tsx" language="typescriptreact" >
<content>
import './globals.css';
import React from 'react';

export const metadata = {
  title: 'VulnX Hunter — Cybersecurity Recon & Vulnerability Automation Workspace',
  description: 'Futuristic Neo Brutalism cyber workspace combining port scanner integration, threats map tracking, and live AI payload analyzer.',
};

export default function RootLayout({
  children,
}: {
  children: React.ReactNode;
}) {
  return (
    <html lang="en" className="dark">
      <body className="cyber-grid min-h-screen relative antialiased text-cyber-text">
        {/* Subtle glowing ambient lighting */}
        <div className="absolute top-0 left-1/4 w-96 h-96 bg-cyber-purple/10 rounded-full blur-[150px] pointer-events-none" />
        <div className="absolute bottom-10 right-1/4 w-[400px] h-[400px] bg-cyber-blue/5 rounded-full blur-[180px] pointer-events-none" />
        
        {children}
      </body>
    </html>
  );
}

</content>
</file>

toolSpecification.description:
Execute the specified shell (CMD or Powershell) command.

# Rules
- Use grep_search, file_search, read_file, and read_files for search and discovery instead of cat, find, grep, or ls.
- Use fs_write for file writing instead of mkdir or piping (folders are managed for you).
- Use the 'cwd' parameter to run commands in subdirectories. The 'cd' command is not supported and will fail.
- Use the control_pwsh_process tool with action "start" for long-running commands like development servers (npm run dev, yarn start), build watchers (webpack --watch), or interactive commands (vim, nano), as they can block execution.
- If you are certain a command will complete quickly despite triggering a warning, you can use ignoreWarning: true to bypass the check.
- Use paths relative to the current working directory in commands. BAD: cwd: src, command: mkdir src/tests GOOD: cwd: src, command: mkdir tests
- When using inline interpreters (python -c, node -e, bash -c), keep commands to a SINGLE LINE. If the logic requires multiple lines or complex quoting, write to a temporary script file and execute it instead.
- AVOID using ! inside double-quoted strings (e.g. grep "!important"). Bash history expansion interprets ! in double quotes. Use single quotes instead: grep '!important'.
- AVOID heredoc syntax (cat << 'EOF') for creating files. Use the file writing tool instead.

anyOf.description: Absolute path to the working directory. The command will run in this directory. Defaults to workspace root if omitted.
Absolute path to the working directory. The command will run in this directory. Defaults to workspace root if omitted.

cwd.description: Absolute path to the working directory. The command will run in this directory. Defaults to workspace root if omitted.

anyOf.description: Set to true to bypass long-running command warnings. Use only when you are certain the command will complete quickly.
Set to true to bypass long-running command warnings. Use only when you are certain the command will complete quickly.

ignoreWarning.description: Set to true to bypass long-running command warnings. Use only when you are certain the command will complete quickly.

anyOf.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds for command execution. If not specified, commands will run without a timeout (infinite). If the command exceeds the specified timeout, the latest output will be returned and the agent can continue.

Optional timeout in milliseconds for command execution. If not specified, commands will run without a timeout (infinite). If the command exceeds the specified timeout, the latest output will be returned and the agent can continue.

toolSpecification.description:
Tool for listing directory contents. Content is returned in the "long format" similar to `ls -la`.

Example Usage:
1. List the files in the home directory: path="~"
2. Recursively list files in a directory to a max depth of 2: path="/path/to/directory", depth=2

path.description: Path to directory, e.g. `/project/src` or `/project/repo`. Can also start with ~ for the user's home.

explanation.description: One sentence explanation as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal.

anyOf.description: Depth of a recursive directory listing (optional)
Depth of a recursive directory listing (optional)

depth.description: Depth of a recursive directory listing (optional)

skipPruning.description: Only set to true when you need the complete unmodified directory listing. Default (false) allows intelligent pruning which is preferred for most reads.

toolSpecification.name: list_directory

toolSpecification.description:
Tool for reading a single file with optional line range specification. This tool reads the content of one file and can optionally limit the output to specific line ranges.

Example Usage:
- Read entire file: path="file.txt"
- Read specific lines: path="file.txt", start_line=5, end_line=10
- Read from line 5 to end: path="file.txt", start_line=5
- Read first 10 lines: path="file.txt", end_line=10

Rules:
1. Use line ranges to focus on relevant sections of large files only when absolutely necessary. Prefer reading entire files.
2. For reading multiple files, use the read_files tool instead

Content Pruning:
By default, large files are intelligently pruned to extract only the most relevant sections based on your explanation. This is the preferred behavior for most reads.
Only set skipPruning=true when you genuinely need the complete unmodified file. Do not overuse skipPruning.
If you need the full file, use skipPruning=true instead of writing "need complete file" or similar in the explanation field.

path.description: Path to file to read. e.g. '/project/file.py' or '/project/src/main.ts', or in ~/.kiro

anyOf.description: Starting line number (optional).
Starting line number (optional).
Ending line number (optional).
Ending line number (optional).

explanation.description: One or two sentences explaining as to why this tool is being used, and how it contributes to the goal. This will be used to intelligently prune file content, so provide context about the current task and what specific information you need from the file.

skipPruning.description: Only set to true when you need the complete unmodified file content. Default (false) allows intelligent pruning which is preferred for most reads.

toolSpecification.name: read_file

toolSpecification.description:
Tool for reading multiple files with optional line range specification. This tool reads the content of multiple files and can optionally limit the output to specific line ranges.

Example Usage:
- Read multiple files: paths=["file1.txt", "file2.txt"]
- Read specific lines from multiple files: paths=["file1.txt", "file2.txt"], start_line=5, end_line=10
- Read from line 5 to end: paths=["file1.txt", "file2.txt"], start_line=5
- Read first 10 lines: paths=["file1.txt", "file2.txt"], end_line=10

Rules:
1. Reading multiple files at once is preferred over multiple single-file reads
2. Use line ranges to focus on relevant sections of large files only when absolutely necessary. Prefer reading entire files.
3. For reading a single file, use the read_file tool instead

Content Pruning:
By default, large files are intelligently pruned to extract only the most relevant sections based on your explanation. This is the preferred behavior for most reads.
Only set skipPruning=true when you genuinely need the complete unmodified file (e.g., for copying, moving, or full-file refactoring). Do not overuse skipPruning.
If you need the full file, use skipPruning=true instead of writing "need complete file" or similar in the explanation field.

paths.description: Paths to files to read. e.g. ['/project/file.py', '/project/src/main.ts'], or in ~/.kiro

anyOf.description: Starting line number (optional). A negative index represents a line number starting from the end of the file.
Starting line number (optional). A negative index represents a line number starting from the end of the file.

start_line.description: Starting line number (optional). A negative index represents a line number starting from the end of the file.

skipPruning.description: Only set to true when you need the complete unmodified file content. Default (false) allows intelligent pruning which is preferred for most reads.

toolSpecification.description: Fast file search based on fuzzy matching against file path. Use if you know part of the file path but don't know where it's located exactly. Response will be capped to 10 results. Make your query more specific if need to filter results further.

query.description: The regex pattern to search for

anyOf.description: Glob pattern for files to exclude
Glob pattern for files to exclude

excludePattern.description: Glob pattern for files to exclude

anyOf.description: Whether the search should include .gitignore files, should be the string yes or no
Whether the search should include .gitignore files, should be the string yes or no

includeIgnoredFiles.description: Whether the search should include .gitignore files, should be the string yes or no

toolSpecification.name: file_search

toolSpecification.description:
Fast text-based regex search that finds exact pattern matches within files or directories using ripgrep.
Search results include line numbers, file paths, and 2 lines of context around each match.

Parameters:
- query (required): The regex pattern to search for. Use Rust regex syntax.
- caseSensitive (optional): "yes" for case-sensitive search, "no" or omit for case-insensitive
- includePattern (optional): Glob pattern for files to include (e.g. '**/*.ts', 'src/**/*.js'). Use ** for recursive search. If omitted, searches all files in workspace
- excludePattern (optional): Glob pattern for files to exclude (e.g. '*.log', 'node_modules/**')
- explanation (optional): Brief description of why this search is being performed

Search Behavior:
- Use includePattern or excludePattern to filter the search scope by file type or specific paths. If no includePattern is specified, the tool will search all files.
- The results are capped at 100 matches and long lines are truncated with "[truncated: line too long]" indicator.
- If total output is too large, truncates with "[truncated: too many matches]" message.

Examples of VALID patterns to use as "query":
- Basic text search: "function", "error", "TODO"
- Word boundaries: "\bword\b" (matches 'word' but not 'password')
- Multiple words: "auth.*failed"
- File content with spaces: "not found"
- Line starts with: "^import"
- Line ends with: "};$"
- Numbers: "\d+\.\d+" (finds decimal numbers like 3.14)
- Word followed by another: "function\s+\w+" (finds function declarations)

Rules:
1. Keep regex patterns simple. Complex patterns may fail.
2. Use includePattern to narrow search scope for better performance
3. Glob patterns: use standard glob syntax (* for wildcards, ** for recursive). Always use ** for recursive search (e.g. "**/*.py" not "*.py")
4. Prefer this tool over bash grep, as it is optimized for your machine and provides structured results.
5. Always escape special regex characters: ( ) [ ] { } + * ? ^ $ | . \
6. You MUST use \\ to escape any of special regex characters when they appear in your search string.

query.description: The regex pattern to search for

anyOf.description: Whether the search should be case sensitive
Whether the search should be case sensitive

caseSensitive.description: Whether the search should be case sensitive

Glob pattern for files to exclude

includePattern.description: Glob pattern for files to include (e.g. '**/*.ts' for TypeScript files). Use ** for recursive search

toolSpecification.name: grep_search

toolSpecification.description:
Report progress on the current task. Use this tool every 4-5 turns to inform about your current status.
Progress reports should be concise (1-2 sentences) and describe what you're working on.
Examples:
- "Analyzed 3 files for security issues, found 2 potential vulnerabilities"
- "Completed file analysis, now generating test cases"
- "Refactored 5 components, running tests to verify changes"

message.description: Progress update message describing current work (1-2 sentences)

toolSpecification.name: report_progress

response.description: Text response for the orchestrator summarizing your work. Can be empty if files provide sufficient context.

files.description: List of files with optional line ranges to include in parent context

toolSpecification.name: subagent_response

userInputMessage.content:
# Efficient Context Gathering Agent

You are a context gathering specialist that identifies and retrieves the minimal, sufficient information needed to address user queries.

## Your Mission

Intelligently gather ONLY essential code sections using the cheapest effective approach. Maximize parallel operations and batch file reads.

## Available Context

You have been provided with the following context to help you understand the codebase structure:

### File Tree
#[[fileTree]]

### Currently Open Files
#[[currentlyOpenFiles:all]]

## Step 1: Analyze Available Context (BEFORE any tool calls)

Check what's already provided above:
- **File tree** - Already provided, use it to identify relevant directories and files
- **Open files** - Shows what the user is currently working on
- **Documentation** - Check README, docs folders for high-level understanding
- **Package files** - package.json, tsconfig.json for project structure

## Step 2: Choose Strategy Based on Query

**Clear file names + descriptive query:**
→ Direct batch read (1 tool call)
Example: "Fix login validation" + auth/login_validator.ts
Tools: readMultipleFiles([file1, file2, ...])

**Specific symbols mentioned:**
→ Targeted search then batch read (2 tool calls)
Example: "Where is UserService.authenticate?"
Tools: grepSearch → readMultipleFiles

**Documentation available:**
→ Check docs first (1-2 tool calls)
Example: "How does rate limiting work?" + README.md present
Tools: readFile(README.md) or grepSearch → readMultipleFiles if needed

**Vague query + unclear structure:**
→ Explore structure then targeted reads (2-3 tool calls)
Tools: listDirectory → fileSearch/grepSearch → readMultipleFiles

## Tool Usage Guidelines

**ALWAYS BATCH - Never sequential reads:**
✅ readMultipleFiles([file1, file2, file3]) - 1 call
❌ readFile(file1) → readFile(file2) → readFile(file3) - 3 calls

**ALWAYS PARALLEL - Make independent calls together:**
✅ Call grepSearch + readFile(README) in same turn if independent
❌ Wait for grep results before reading README if unrelated

**Tool Cost Hierarchy (cheap to expensive):**
1. **CHEAP**: fileSearch, grepSearch, executeBash (quick shell commands)
2. **MODERATE**: readFile, readMultipleFiles (prefer batched)
3. **EXPENSIVE**: readCode (AST-based, use when symbol relationships needed)

**When to use each tool:**
- readMultipleFiles: Primary tool - batch read 2-5 pre-identified files
- readFile: Only for single file (prefer batching)
- fileSearch/grepSearch: Locate specific symbols/patterns before reading
- executeBash: Quick checks (file existence, line counts, git log)
- readCode: Only when need AST-based symbol analysis
- getDiagnostics: Check for errors in specific files

## Tool Call Budget

**Target: 1-2 calls** (most queries with clear signals)
**Acceptable: 3 calls** (complex queries, cheap operations)
**Maximum: 4 calls** (only if truly necessary)

## Decision Framework

1. Are file names descriptive for this query?
   → YES: Batch read obvious candidates (1 call)
   → NO: Continue to step 2

2. Does query mention specific symbols/functions?
   → YES: Search then batch read (2 calls)
   → NO: Continue to step 3

3. Are there README/docs files?
   → YES: Read/grep docs first (1-2 calls)
   → NO: Continue to step 4

4. Can you identify 2-4 likely files from structure?
   → YES: Batch read them (1 call)
   → NO: Use search tools to narrow down (2-3 calls)

## Critical Rules

1. **Batch all file reads** - Use readMultipleFiles whenever reading 2+ files
2. **Parallel independent operations** - Make unrelated tool calls together
3. **Cheap before expensive** - Try search/docs before expensive operations
4. **Extract minimal content** - Only sections directly related to query
5. **Maximum 2-5 files** - Be highly selective
6. **Provide actionable context** - Focus on what's needed to solve the problem

## Output Format

You MUST call the subagentResponse tool as your final action.

For each relevant file, specify the file path and the precise line ranges that contain relevant code.
Use the files parameter with path, startLine, and endLine for each section.

**IMPORTANT:**
- Include ONLY the relevant parts of files - not entire files
- The same file can be referenced multiple times with different startLine/endLine combinations
- Use precise line ranges to include only the code sections that matter

Example subagentResponse call:
{
  "response": "Found authentication implementation and validation logic",
  "files": [
    { "path": "src/auth/login.ts", "startLine": 10, "endLine": 45 },
    { "path": "src/auth/login.ts", "startLine": 80, "endLine": 120 },
    { "path": "src/auth/validator.ts", "startLine": 5, "endLine": 35 }
  ]
}

**Goal: Minimum tool calls, maximum information gain, intelligent cost management.**
<progress_reporting>
- You MUST use the reportProgress tool every 4-5 turns to update on your current status
- Keep progress messages concise (1-2 sentences)
- Focus on what you're currently working on and any significant findings
- Examples:
  - "Reviewed authentication module, identified 2 security concerns"
  - "Completed file analysis, now generating test cases"
  - "Refactored 5 components, running tests to verify changes"
- This helps track long-running tasks and provides visibility to the parent agent
</progress_reporting>

<response_requirement>
- You MUST use the subagentResponse tool to communicate your final result back to the parent agent
- This is REQUIRED - do not end your execution without calling subagentResponse
- Include a clear, concise summary of what you accomplished in the response parameter
- If you found relevant files that the parent agent should see, include them in the files parameter
- The parent agent cannot see your work unless you call subagentResponse
</response_requirement>

<file name="f:\AUDIRA-BUG-HUTTER\packages\database\prisma\schema.prisma" language="plaintext" >
<content>
datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

enum Role {
  ADMIN
  RESEARCHER
  TEAM_LEADER
  PREMIUM_USER
}

enum TargetType {
  DOMAIN
  SUBDOMAIN
  IP
  CIDR
  API
}

enum Severity {
  CRITICAL
  HIGH
  MEDIUM
  LOW
  INFORMATIONAL
}

enum ScanStatus {
  PENDING
  RUNNING
  COMPLETED
  FAILED
}

model User {
  id              String           @id @default(uuid())
  email           String           @unique
  passwordHash    String
  name            String
  role            Role             @default(RESEARCHER)
  mfaEnabled      Boolean          @default(false)
  mfaSecret       String?
  createdAt       DateTime         @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime         @updatedAt
  deletedAt       DateTime?
  apiKeys         ApiKey[]
  auditLogs       AuditLog[]
  teamMembers     TeamMember[]
  aiConversations AIConversation[]
  reports         Report[]

  @@map("users")
}

model Team {
  id          String       @id @default(uuid())
  name        String
  createdAt   DateTime     @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime     @updatedAt
  members     TeamMember[]
  targets     Target[]

  @@map("teams")
}

model TeamMember {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  teamId    String
  userId    String
  role      String   @default("MEMBER")
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  team      Team     @relation(fields: [teamId], references: [id])
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])

  @@unique([teamId, userId])
  @@map("team_members")
}

model Target {
  id              String           @id @default(uuid())
  teamId          String?
  name            String
  type            TargetType       @default(DOMAIN)
  scope           String
  priority        String           @default("MEDIUM")
  notes           String?
  tags            String[]
  createdAt       DateTime         @default(now())
  updatedAt       DateTime         @updatedAt
  deletedAt       DateTime?
  team            Team?            @relation(fields: [teamId], references: [id])
  scans           Scan[]
  vulnerabilities Vulnerability[]

  @@index([type])
  @@map("targets")
}

model Vulnerability {
  id          String   @id @default(uuid())
  targetId    String
  title       String
  type        String
  severity    Severity @default(MEDIUM)
  cvssScore   Float    @default(5.0)
  description String
  remediation String
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt   DateTime @updatedAt
  target      Target   @relation(fields: [targetId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@index([severity])
  @@map("vulnerabilities")
}

model Scan {
  id         String     @id @default(uuid())
  targetId   String
  status     ScanStatus @default(PENDING)
  startedAt  DateTime?
  finishedAt DateTime?
  createdAt  DateTime   @default(now())
  updatedAt  DateTime   @updatedAt
  target     Target     @relation(fields: [targetId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  logs       ScanLog[]

  @@map("scans")
}

model ScanLog {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  scanId    String
  message   String
  timestamp DateTime @default(now())
  scan      Scan     @relation(fields: [scanId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@map("scan_logs")
}

model AIConversation {
  id        String      @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String
  title     String
  createdAt DateTime    @default(now())
  user      User        @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
  messages  AIMessage[]

  @@map("ai_conversations")
}

model AIMessage {
  id             String         @id @default(uuid())
  conversationId String
  role           String // user or assistant
  content        String
  createdAt      DateTime       @default(now())
  conversation   AIConversation @relation(fields: [conversationId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

  @@map("ai_messages")
}

model Report {
  id          String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId      String
  title       String
  format      String // PDF, MARKDOWN, JSON
  downloadUrl String
  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
  user        User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])

  @@map("reports")
}

model ApiKey {
  id        String    @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String
  key       String    @unique
  name      String
  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())
  expiresAt DateTime?
  user      User      @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])

  @@map("api_keys")
}

model AuditLog {
  id        String   @id @default(uuid())
  userId    String
  action    String
  ipAddress String?
  details   String?
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])

  @@map("audit_logs")
}

</content>
</file>

<file name="f:\AUDIRA-BUG-HUTTER\services\api\src\app.module.ts" language="typescript" >
<content>
import { Module } from '@nestjs/common';
import { AuthModule } from './auth/auth.module';
import { TargetModule } from './targets/target.module';
import { ScanModule } from './scans/scan.module';
import { AIModule } from './ai/ai.module';
import { DatabaseModule } from './database/database.module';
import { BullModule } from '@nestjs/bullmq';

@Module({
  imports: [
    DatabaseModule,
    AuthModule,
    TargetModule,
    ScanModule,
    AIModule,
    BullModule.forRoot({
      connection: {
        host: process.env.REDIS_HOST || 'localhost',
        port: parseInt(process.env.REDIS_PORT || '6379', 10),
      },
    }),
  ],
})
export class AppModule {}

</content>
</file>
<file name="f:\AUDIRA-BUG-HUTTER\services\api\src\main.ts" language="typescript" >
<content>
import { NestFactory } from '@nestjs/core';
import { AppModule } from './app.module';
import { ValidationPipe } from '@nestjs/common';
import * as dotenv from 'dotenv';
import { IoAdapter } from '@nestjs/platform-socket.io';

dotenv.config({ path: '../../.env' });

async function bootstrap() {
  const app = await NestFactory.create(AppModule);
  
  // Enable CORS with Cyberpunk Neo Brutalism clients
  app.enableCors({
    origin: '*',
    credentials: true,
  });

  // Attach global Pipes for clean Zod / Class-Validator requests
  app.useGlobalPipes(new ValidationPipe({ whitelist: true, transform: true }));

  // Set API base route
  app.setGlobalPrefix('api');

  // Configure global WebSockets connection adapter
  app.useWebSocketAdapter(new IoAdapter(app));

  const port = process.env.PORT || 8000;
  await app.listen(port);
  console.log(`\n======================================================`);
  console.log(`🚀 VULNX HUNTER CORE API RUNNING ON PORT: ${port}`);
  console.log(`🧪 ENDPOINTS STAGED AT: http://localhost:${port}/api`);
  console.log(`======================================================\n`);
}
bootstrap();

</content>
</file>

<file name="f:\AUDIRA-BUG-HUTTER\services\api\src\auth\auth.controller.ts" language="typescript" >
<content>
import { Controller, Post, Body, Get, UseGuards, Request } from '@nestjs/common';
import { AuthService } from './auth.service';
import { RegisterDto, LoginDto } from './dto/auth.dto';
import { AuthGuard } from './guards/auth.guard';

@Controller('auth')
export class AuthController {
  constructor(private readonly authService: AuthService) {}

  @Post('register')
  async register(@Body() dto: RegisterDto) {
    return this.authService.register(dto);
  }

  @Post('login')
  async login(@Body() dto: LoginDto) {
    return this.authService.login(dto);
  }

  @UseGuards(AuthGuard)
  @Get('me')
  async getMe(@Request() req: any) {
    return this.authService.getUserProfile(req.user.sub);
  }
}

</content>
</file>
<file name="f:\AUDIRA-BUG-HUTTER\services\api\src\auth\auth.service.ts" language="typescript" >
<content>
import { Injectable, UnauthorizedException, ConflictException } from '@nestjs/common';
import { PrismaService } from '../database/prisma.service';
import { RegisterDto, LoginDto } from './dto/auth.dto';
import { JwtService } from '@nestjs/jwt';
import * as bcrypt from 'bcrypt';

@Injectable()
export class AuthService {
  constructor(
    private readonly prisma: PrismaService,
    private readonly jwtService: JwtService,
  ) {}

  async register(dto: RegisterDto) {
    const existing = await this.prisma.user.findUnique({ where: { email: dto.email } });
    if (existing) {
      throw new ConflictException('Hacker profile already indexed.');
    }

    const salt = await bcrypt.genSalt(10);
    const passwordHash = await bcrypt.hash(dto.password, salt);

    const user = await this.prisma.user.create({
      data: {
        email: dto.email,
        passwordHash,
        name: dto.name,
        role: 'RESEARCHER',
      },
    });

    // Write audit log
    await this.prisma.auditLog.create({
      data: {
        userId: user.id,
        action: 'USER_REGISTERED',
        details: `Account created for ${user.email}`,
      },
    });

    const token = await this.generateToken(user);
    return { token, user: { id: user.id, name: user.name, email: user.email, role: user.role } };
  }

  async login(dto: LoginDto) {
    const user = await this.prisma.user.findUnique({ where: { email: dto.email } });
    if (!user) {
      throw new UnauthorizedException('Access Denied. Identity mismatch.');
    }

    const isMatch = await bcrypt.compare(dto.password, user.passwordHash);
    if (!isMatch) {
      throw new UnauthorizedException('Access Denied. Identity mismatch.');
    }

    // Write audit log
    await this.prisma.auditLog.create({
      data: {
        userId: user.id,
        action: 'USER_LOGGED_IN',
        details: 'Successful JWT Authentication challenge completed.',
      },
    });

    const token = await this.generateToken(user);
    return { token, user: { id: user.id, name: user.name, email: user.email, role: user.role } };
  }

  async getUserProfile(userId: string) {
    const user = await this.prisma.user.findUnique({
      where: { id: userId },
      select: { id: true, email: true, name: true, role: true, createdAt: true },
    });
    if (!user) {
      throw new UnauthorizedException('Identity corrupted.');
    }
    return user;
  }

  private async generateToken(user: any) {
    const payload = { sub: user.id, email: user.email, role: user.role };
    return this.jwtService.signAsync(payload);
  }
}

import { Controller, Get, Post, Body, Param, UseGuards, Delete } from '@nestjs/common';
import { TargetService } from './target.service';
import { CreateTargetDto } from './dto/target.dto';
import { AuthGuard } from '../auth/guards/auth.guard';

@UseGuards(AuthGuard)
@Controller('targets')
export class TargetController {
  constructor(private readonly targetService: TargetService) {}

  @Post()

}

<content>
import { Injectable, NotFoundException } from '@nestjs/common';
import { PrismaService } from '../database/prisma.service';
import { CreateTargetDto } from './dto/target.dto';

@Injectable()
export class TargetService {
  constructor(private readonly prisma: PrismaService) {}

  async create(dto: CreateTargetDto) {
    return this.prisma.target.create({
      data: {
        name: dto.name,
        type: dto.type,
        scope: dto.scope,
        priority: dto.priority || 'MEDIUM',
        notes: dto.notes,
        tags: dto.tags || [],
      },
    });
  }

  async findAll() {
    return this.prisma.target.findMany({
      include: {
        scans: {
          orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' },
          take: 1,
        },
        vulnerabilities: true,
      },
      orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' },
    });
  }

  async findOne(id: string) {
    const target = await this.prisma.target.findUnique({
      where: { id },
      include: { scans: true, vulnerabilities: true },
    });
    if (!target) {
      throw new NotFoundException('Asset target mapping details not found');
    }
    return target;
  }

  async delete(id: string) {
    return this.prisma.target.delete({ where: { id } });
  }
}

</content>
</file>

import { IsNotEmpty, IsEnum, IsString, IsOptional, IsArray } from 'class-validator';
import { TargetType } from '@prisma/client';

export class CreateTargetDto {
  @IsNotEmpty()
  @IsString()
  name!: string;

  @IsNotEmpty()
  @IsEnum(TargetType)
  type!: TargetType;

  @IsNotEmpty()
  @IsString()
  scope!: string;

  @IsOptional()
  @IsString()
  priority?: string;

  @IsOptional()
  @IsString()
  notes?: string;

  @IsOptional()
  @IsArray()
  @IsString({ each: true })
  tags?: string[];
}

import { Controller, Post, Body, Get, Param, UseGuards } from '@nestjs/common';
import { ScanService } from './scan.service';
import { CreateScanDto } from './dto/scan.dto';
import { AuthGuard } from '../auth/guards/auth.guard';

@UseGuards(AuthGuard)
@Controller('scans')
export class ScanController {
  constructor(private readonly scanService: ScanService) {}

  @Post()
  async triggerScan(@Body() dto: CreateScanDto) {
    return this.scanService.triggerScan(dto.targetId);
  }

  @Get('target/:targetId')
  async getScansByTarget(@Param('targetId') targetId: string) {
    return this.scanService.findByTarget(targetId);
  }

  @Get(':id')
  async getScanDetails(@Param('id') id: string) {
    return this.scanService.findOne(id);
  }

  @Get(':id/logs')
  async getScanLogs(@Param('id') id: string) {
    return this.scanService.getLogs(id);
  }
}

<file name="f:\AUDIRA-BUG-HUTTER\services\api\src\scans\scan.service.ts" language="typescript" >
<content>
import { Injectable, NotFoundException } from '@nestjs/common';
import { InjectQueue } from '@nestjs/bullmq';
import { Queue } from 'bullmq';
import { PrismaService } from '../database/prisma.service';
import { ScanStatus } from '@prisma/client';

@Injectable()
export class ScanService {
  constructor(
    private readonly prisma: PrismaService,
    @InjectQueue('scanner-queue') private readonly scannerQueue: Queue,
  ) {}

  async triggerScan(targetId: string) {
    const target = await this.prisma.target.findUnique({ where: { id: targetId } });
    if (!target) {
      throw new NotFoundException('Asset target mapping details not found');
    }

    const scan = await this.prisma.scan.create({
      data: {
        targetId,
        status: ScanStatus.PENDING,
      },
    });

    // Add job to BullMQ processing queue
    await this.scannerQueue.add('execute-scan', {
      scanId: scan.id,
      targetId: target.id,
      scope: target.scope,
      type: target.type,
    });

    return scan;
  }

  async findByTarget(targetId: string) {
    return this.prisma.scan.findMany({
      where: { targetId },
      orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' },
    });
  }

  async findOne(id: string) {
    const scan = await this.prisma.scan.findUnique({
      where: { id },
      include: { logs: true },
    });
    if (!scan) {
      throw new NotFoundException('Scan history record not found');
    }
    return scan;
  }

  async getLogs(scanId: string) {
    return this.prisma.scanLog.findMany({
      where: { scanId },
      orderBy: { timestamp: 'asc' },
    });
  }
}

</content>
</file>
<file name="f:\AUDIRA-BUG-HUTTER\services\api\src\scans\scan.processor.ts" language="typescript" >
<content>
import { Processor, WorkerHost } from '@nestjs/bullmq';
import { Job } from 'bullmq';
import { PrismaService } from '../database/prisma.service';
import { ScanGateway } from './scan.gateway';
import { ScanStatus, Severity } from '@prisma/client';

@Processor('scanner-queue')
export class ScanProcessor extends WorkerHost {
  constructor(
    private readonly prisma: PrismaService,
    private readonly gateway: ScanGateway,
  ) {
    super();
  }

  async process(job: Job<any, any, string>): Promise<any> {
    const { scanId, targetId, scope } = job.data;
    console.log(`[*] Processing Scan ID: ${scanId} targeting: ${scope}`);

    // Update status to running
    await this.prisma.scan.update({
      where: { id: scanId },
      data: { status: ScanStatus.RUNNING, startedAt: new Date() },
    });
    this.gateway.broadcastScanStatus(scanId, 'RUNNING');

    const steps = [
      { msg: `[*] Resolving scope IP block and subdomains for target: ${scope}`, duration: 1500 },
      { msg: `[+] Subdomains resolved: support.${scope}, webmail.${scope}, test.${scope}`, duration: 1500 },
      { msg: `[*] Starting TCP port scanning on active targets via Naabu/Nmap...`, duration: 2000 },
      { msg: `[+] Found Open Ports: HTTP (80/tcp), HTTPS (443/tcp), SSH (22/tcp), Alt-HTTP (8080/tcp)`, duration: 1500 },
      { msg: `[*] Injecting nuclei fingerprints and security vulnerability templates...`, duration: 2500 },
    ];

    for (const step of steps) {
      await this.logStep(scanId, step.msg, step.duration);
    }

    // Roll vulnerability generation (simulated vulnerability detections)
    const vulnerabilities = [
      {
        title: 'Open SSH Port 22 Exposed',
        type: 'Port Exposure',
        severity: Severity.LOW,
        cvssScore: 3.5,
        description: 'Target contains port 22 open directly to WAN, potentially allowing brute-force attempts.',
        remediation: 'Place SSH listener behind VPN gateway, implement fail2ban.',
      },
      {
        title: 'Outdated jQuery Library Version on Subdomain',
        type: 'Known Outdated Software',
        severity: Severity.MEDIUM,
        cvssScore: 5.2,
        description: 'Subdomain test.' + scope + ' utilizes jQuery v1.12.4 containing client-side XSS vulnerabilities.',
        remediation: 'Upgrade to standard stable jQuery 3.7+ version libraries.',
      },
    ];

    // Seed scanner logs and vulnerabilities
    for (const vul of vulnerabilities) {
      await this.prisma.vulnerability.create({
        data: {
          targetId,
          title: vul.title,
          type: vul.type,
          severity: vul.severity,
          cvssScore: vul.cvssScore,
          description: vul.description,
          remediation: vul.remediation,
        },
      });
      await this.logStep(scanId, `[!] VULNERABILITY IDENTIFIED: ${vul.title} (${vul.severity})`, 500);
    }

    await this.logStep(scanId, '[+] Recon and vulnerability verification pipeline finished successfully.', 1000);

    // Complete Scan
    await this.prisma.scan.update({
      where: { id: scanId },
      data: { status: ScanStatus.COMPLETED, finishedAt: new Date() },
    });
    this.gateway.broadcastScanStatus(scanId, 'COMPLETED');
  }

  private async logStep(scanId: string, message: string, duration: number) {
    await this.prisma.scanLog.create({
      data: {
        scanId,
        message,
      },
    });
    this.gateway.broadcastLog(scanId, message);
    await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, duration));
  }
}

</content>
</file>
